bio_interpretation: Ward-linkage clustering cleanly separates genomic lineages (within=11 vs between=366 SNP, 32.2× separation). ARI=0.128: partial correspondence with NCBI SNP cluster labels.

Explicit SNP alignment (F1=0.418) outperforms general DNABERT embeddings (F1=0.344). Without Salmonella-specific fine-tuning, DNABERT representations are insufficiently specialised for source attribution on this small dataset.

SNP features show partial source signal (Macro F1=0.418). Class imbalance or genomic similarity across certain source groups limits consistent classification. Partial overlap between food and food-animal classes is biologically expected given shared supply-chain transmission routes.
Hybrid performance (F1=0.355) falls below individual modes. High-dimensional concatenation may introduce noise in this small-sample regime; dimensionality reduction before concatenation could help.
K-mer frequency (F1=0.595) is competitive with alignment-based features, indicating that global nucleotide composition already encodes source-relevant genomic signatures without alignment.
